events.title: Library Yarns stitch group
events.description: If you enjoy knitting, crochet, embroidery or any other portable needle/fiber craft, please join us at our new twice-monthly, drop-in stitching group hosted by the Verona Public Library. Meet up with other fiber art enthusiasts, learn new techniques, or find inspiration for your next project!

events.title: Crisp and Green Yarn Crafting
events.description:
This is a very normal yarn crafting meetup where we gather once a week to chat a bit and work on yarn/craft projects outside our house with IRL people. If that sounds fun, come and join us! We'll be at Crisp and Green, which serves salads as big as your head (among other things).

All ages and stages are welcome, but if you're coming to learn, please mention it in the comments so someone is prepared to teach.

events.title: HWY 1 Yarn Crafting
events.description:
This week we'll be meeting at the excellent Burrito place HWY 1. It's in the old Miko Poke location, and since Meetup hasn't updated their maps since 2019 or so, it doesn't have the updated restaurant name, but the address should still get you there. Tables are very normal at HWY 1, and we meet there during happy hour (a happy coincidence!), which means half off drinks or nachos!

So grab your latest greatest handiwork project, and come hang out with us for an evening!

events.title: Library Yarns Stitch group
events.description:
If you enjoy knitting, crochet, embroidery, or another fiber or needlecraft, please join us at a (mostly) weekly, drop-in stitching circle hosted by the library.

Meet up with other fiber art enthusiasts, learn new techniques, exchange ideas, or find inspiration for your next project.

In warmer weather,, we may meet outside on the lawn behind the library. Bring a lawn chair and your project. In case of cold or rainy (or too hot!) weather, the group will meet in the library’s Conference Room....

events.title: Forage Kitchen Yarn Crafting
events.description:
Greetings!

It has come to my attention that there's outdoor seating at Hilldale, which means we can get food at whatever place there, and then just sit outside and eat! I'm leaving Forage Kitchen in the name, since that's the rain location, and also a great option--but there's plenty of other delicious places to grab food in Hilldale mall (though sadly not the dumpling house which closed last month). I'll try to get a table near Forage Kitchen, which is also the cut through/alley/crevice/wha...

events.title: Beginner's Workshop
events.description:
Looking to start a yarn craft, but not sure where to begin?

Then this, dear potential friend, is the meetup for you!

This is one of our occasional Beginner's Workshops, which are set up mostly like our normal meetups, except that some of us are ready and willing to teach new people new things, or guide people through relearning an old skill. It's also just a great chance to work on a hobby around other people with similar interests.

All we ask of new yarn crafters is that you bring your ow...

events.title: Panera Yarn Crafting
events.description:
Hello friends,

This week we're going to patronize the Panera Bread on West Washington. They serve delicious sandwiches and overpriced cookies, as you probably know. Anyway, we are going to meet there, and bring our yarn and get all tangled and untangled in social webs and yarn webs for an evening before going home and realizing we did the last six rows of our (actually very simple!) lace shawl wrong, but also we won't care because we had fun chatting with our friends (both old and new) along...

events.title: Jason's Deli Yarn Crafting
events.description:
This week, we are meeting at Jason's Deli, which is a neat little all-around diner. They have lots of little tables we can pull together, and free soft serve ice cream, so it's a great place for us to meet.

If you'd like to join us, just bring your current or most portable craft project (most of us knit or crochet, but seriously you can bring whatever, we're not exclusionary), and a curiosity about making new friends!

events.title: Sweetgreen Yarn Crafting
events.description:
Hello Friends!

Alright. This is again more of a "Hilldale Yarn Crafting" than Sweetgreen, but Sweetgreen remains the rain location. If it's not raining, we'll be sitting outside at a table so people can grab food from whatever restaurant they'd like--Hilldale has a lot of good options, even if they don't fully work for our normal style of meetups. Go grab some yarn and come find us and knit with us and be friends with us.

All ages and stages are welcome.

events.title: Chocolate Shoppe Yarn Crafting
events.description:
I'm scheduling this barely in June, so we're very much gambling on the weather here, but assuming it's nice out (and society hasn't collapsed or whatever), We Are Going To Meet At The Chocolate Shoppe Today!!! Woo!

events.title: Food Truck Friday at Garver Feed Mill
events.description: 1. Hello everyone! Please join me for another fun, casual evening out, this time at Garver Feed Mill in the Atwood Neighborhood. This place is so much fun with ample seating, a great selection of food and live music. Look for me where the band is, hopefully I’ll be able to score the comfy sofa area. If not, I’ll try and get us a table in the same area. This is a rain or shine event. If there is bad weather we can play games at Roll Play, the Korean restaurant inside. Hope to see you there!

events.title: After Hours Olbrich Botanical Gardens
events.description: Hello ladies! This is my first event as substitute organizer and I hope you can join me at the Olbrich Botanical Gardens. During the summer, they have a special after hours events every Tuesday for the public, you do not have to be a member to attend. You still need a ticket, they are free, click on this link to get one: https://www.olbrich.org/calendar/after-hours. Please join me, have a glass of wine and enjoy live music in this beautiful setting!

events.title: Pontoon boat ride
events.description: The Madison School and Community Recreation (MSCR) offers drop in Pontoon boat rides at various locations during the summer months. On occasion, Michelle from our group and her husband operate these boats on a volunteer basis. Rides cost $5 and you pay in person on location when you register. Boat capacity is only 15 people and tickets are not available in advance. Actual ride is 90 minutes and starts at 1:30 pm. Ticket sales start 45 minutes ahead of time and I will be there at 12:30 pm. The...

events.title: Album Club: Michael Jackson, Off the Wall (1979)
events.description:
Many of us know that *Thriller* (#12 on the Top 100) made Michael Jackson the biggest pop star on earth. But three years before that masterpiece, there was *Off the Wall* (#36 on the Top 100).

Released in 1979, *Off the Wall* was Michael Jackson's first full collaboration with producer Quincy Jones, and together they created something that didn't quite fit any existing category. It was funky, polished, and joyful. And from what I've read, the music establishment at the time didn't quite know...

events.title: Movie Club: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1937)
events.description:
With pick #34 on the Top 100, we travel back to 1937 and to something that had genuinely never existed before in America: a feature-length animated film. Walt Disney's gamble was so widely doubted that Hollywood called it "Disney's Folly."

Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs is the story we all think we know: a princess, a wicked queen, a poisoned apple, a glass coffin. But people who've revisited it are often surprised by how strange and dark it still feels — how genuinely menacing the Queen is...

events.title: Book Club (FTP Edition): Parable of the Talents, Octavia Butler
events.description:
We first met Lauren Olamina in *Parable of the Sower*, and her vision and determination stayed with me for weeks. *Parable of the Talents* picks up right where we left off, and somehow Octavia Butler makes the stakes even higher.

Lauren has made it out of the chaos of *Sower* with a small community, a shared belief system she calls Earthseed, and a fragile sense of hope.

The America of *Talents* has found its strongman — a politician running on "Make America Great Again" (!) whose followers...

events.title: Book Discussion: Before the Coffee Gets Cold by Toshikazu Kawaguchi
events.description:
**Before the Coffee Gets Cold by Toshikazu Kawaguchi**

**We're going to try something new for this discussion:** we'll meet at Lakeside St Coffee House, then walk along the lake as we talk!

\*\*\*What would you change if you could go back in time? \*\*\*

In a small back alley in Tokyo, there is a café which has been serving carefully brewed coffee for more than one hundred years. But this coffee shop offers its customers a unique experience: the chance to travel back in time.

In *Before t...

events.title: Book Discussion: There is No Antimemetics Division by qntm
events.description:
**There is No Antimemetics Division by qntm**

**Humanity is under assault by malevolent "antimemes"—ideas that attack memory, identity, and the fabric of reality itself—in this wickedly brainbending tale of science-fiction horror, an entirely reimagined and expanded version of the beloved online novel.**

They’re all around us, hiding in plain sight.

One could be in the room with you, now, just to your left. You could be *seeing* it right now—but from this second to the next, you’ll forget ...

events.title: Book Discussion: Automatic Noodle by Annalee Newitz
events.description:
**Automatic Noodle by Annalee Newitz**

**From sci-fi visionary and acclaimed author Annalee Newitz comes Automatic Noodle, a cozy near-future novella about a crew of abandoned food service bots opening their very own restaurant.**

While San Francisco rebuilds from the chaos of war, a group of food service bots in an abandoned ghost kitchen take over their own delivery app account. They rebrand as a neighborhood lunch spot and start producing some of the tastiest hand-pulled noodles in the c...

events.title: Book Discussion: Ancillary Justice by Ann Leckie
events.description:
**Ancillary Justice by Ann Leckie**

On a remote, icy planet, the soldier known as Breq is drawing closer to completing her quest.

Once, she was the Justice of Toren - a colossal starship with an artificial intelligence linking thousands of soldiers in the service of the Radch, the empire that conquered the galaxy.

Now, an act of treachery has ripped it all away, leaving her with one fragile human body, unanswered questions, and a burning desire for vengeance.

events.title: Book Discussion: A Memory Called Empire by Arkady Martine
events.description:
**A Memory Called Empire by Arkady Martine**

Ambassador Mahit Dzmare arrives in the center of the multi-system Teixcalaanli Empire only to discover that her predecessor, the previous ambassador from their small but fiercely independent mining Station, has died. But no one will admit that his death wasn't an accident―or that Mahit might be next to die, during a time of political instability in the highest echelons of the imperial court.

Now, Mahit must discover who is behind the murder, resc...

events.title: Book Discussion: Dreadful by Caitlin Rozakis
events.description:
**Dreadful by Caitlin Rozakis**

**A sharp-witted, high fantasy farce featuring killer moat squid, toxic masculinity, evil wizards and a garlic festival - all at once. Perfect for fans of T. Kingfisher, K. J. Parker and Travis Baldree.**

It’s bad enough waking up in a half-destroyed evil wizard’s workshop with no eyebrows, no memories, and no idea how long you have before the Dread Lord Whomever shows up to murder you horribly and then turn your skull into a goblet or something.

It’s a lot ...

events.title: Book Discussion: The Master and Margarita by Mikhail Bulgakov
events.description:
**The Master and Margarita by Mikhail Bulgakov**

An audacious revision of the stories of Faust and Pontius Pilate, *The Master and Margarita* is recognized as one of the essential classics of modern Russian literature. The novel's vision of Soviet life in the 1930s is so ferociously accurate that it could not be published during its author's lifetime and appeared only in a censored edition in the 1960s. Its truths are so enduring that its language has become part of the common Russian speech...

events.title: Shut Up & Write!® in Beaver Dam
events.description:
Join us for some focused writing time. All writers and all experience levels are welcome and don’t worry, no one will see what you've written. This session is about getting your writing done and meeting other writers in your area.

When you show up, say hello to everyone, grab a drink, and get your writing tools ready. Once you’re all settled, take some time to chat with your fellow writers. Once everyone’s arrived, I will lead quick introductions and then set the timer for our quiet and focu...

events.title: Shut Up & Write! Madison Central Library
events.description:
**Where:** Madison Central Library, Room 209
**When:** Every Saturday, 10:00 AM

Join us for an hour of writing! We’ve discovered that it’s strikingly helpful to write with other writers. See if it’s true for you. Be it a book, blog, script, essay, dissertation, resume, melody, poem or just plain work stuff, you are invited to write it with us. No one will see what you've written or give you unsolicited advice. Instead of just thinking about writing, come and get some words on the page.

event.title: Trivia Night at The Lone Girl Brewing Company
event.description: Your Weekly Bar Trivia Challenge! Drink a beer, munch some food, and compete for glory & prizes! FREE to play!

editors_picks.commentary: Bring your appetite (and maybe a friend).

event.title: Hawthorne Library Contemporary Horror Book Club: "American Rapture" by CJ Lead
event.description: Calling all bookish scream queens! Join us for reflective and spirited talks on today’s most possessing horror literature. We’ll explore themes of grief, memory, madness, revenge, faith, and…

editors_picks.commentary: Madison at its best -- people showing up for each other.

event.title: 🥙 Cook & Connect: Mediterranean Bowl Night 🥙
event.description:
Join us for our first Cook & Connect community dinner! Instead of meeting at a restaurant, we'll gather in the community kitchen to prepare a meal together, share conversation, and enjoy a relaxed midweek evening.

For this month's dinner, we'll be making Mediterranean bowls with a variety of proteins, toppings, and sides so everyone can build a bowl they enjoy. Whether you're a confident cook or have never chopped a cucumber in your life, you're welcome here 😊

event.title: Bernie's Beach Outdoor Yoga
event.description:
Taught by: Marcella Speich from Backyard Yoga

This Meetup will be at Bernie's Beach Park, behind Lakeside Coffee Shop

Please bring a yoga mat or towel, water, and whatever else you need to make it your best practice. There is a general waiver to practice so please either fill one out ahead of time or come early enough to fill one out before class. The instructor usually arrives about 15 mins early.
